sanskrit:
अश्वत्थः सर्ववृक्षाणां देवर्षीणां च नारदः।
गन्धर्वाणां चित्ररथः सिद्धानां कपिलो मुनिः॥
transliteration: aśvatthaḥ sarva-vṛkṣāṇāṃ devarṣīṇāṃ ca nāradaḥ | gandharvāṇāṃ citrarathaḥ siddhānāṃ kapilo muniḥ ||
literal_meaning: Among all trees I am the sacred fig; among celestial sages, Narada; among Gandharvas, Citraratha; among perfected beings, the sage Kapila.
contextual_meaning: Krishna names four archetypal excellences—natural, devotional, artistic, and contemplative—to reassure Arjuna that every domain of life already contains a living embodiment of the Divine. Excellence itself is a mirror of the Self.
psychological_state: Arjuna experiences a moment of cognitive expansion: if every field has its unsurpassed exemplar, then his own warrior path can also be lived as a sacred vocation.
trigger_scenario: Scrolling LinkedIn at 2 a.m., seeing peers crowned 'Top 30 under 30,' and wondering whether your own ordinary life still matters.
anchor_text: Whatever you excel at, let it be your mirror of the Divine.
